guides.title: How to Use AI in Engineering & Dev
guides.intro: AI‑driven code assistants now write boiler‑plate, surface regressions, and auto‑scale infra definitions, shaving hours from daily dev cycles. Teams that embed LLMs in CI/CD, testing, and architecture reviews see faster delivery and fewer manual bugs.

steps.title: Generate Boilerplate via Prompt
steps.body: Open a new branch and run a local LLM (e.g., Claude or Code Llama) with a concise prompt describing the component, API contract, or microservice. Accept the output, run the IDE’s formatter, and push – you have a working scaffold in minutes.

steps.title: AI‑Assist Pull‑Request Review
steps.body: Configure a GitHub Action that sends each PR diff to an LLM for style, security, and performance hints, then posts the summary as a comment. Use the comment to triage obvious issues before a human review.

steps.title: Auto‑Generate CI Pipelines
steps.body: Feed your repo’s tech stack (e.g., Node + Docker) to an LLM prompt that returns a ready‑to‑use GitHub Actions YAML. Paste the YAML, commit, and watch the pipeline spin up without hand‑crafting scripts.

steps.title: Create Test Suites Instantly
steps.body: Run an LLM against your recent feature branch asking for unit and end‑to‑end tests in your preferred framework (Jest, Playwright, etc.). Paste the generated tests, run them locally, and iterate on flaky cases.

steps.title: Add AI‑Powered Monitoring
steps.body: Deploy a lightweight log‑analysis agent that forwards metrics to an LLM endpoint, which flags anomalous patterns (latency spikes, error bursts) in Slack. Set the alert threshold once and let the model refine it over time.

pro_tips: Version‑control your prompts; a small wording tweak can halve review comment noise.
Guard LLM outputs with static analysis – run ESLint or bandit before merging any AI‑generated code.
Fine‑tune a small internal model on your codebase for higher accuracy and lower data‑leak risk.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Marketing
guides.intro: AI is now crunching your performance data, generating copy, and even designing ad visuals fast enough to outpace weekly sprint cycles. The result is a tighter feedback loop between audience signals and creative output, letting marketers iterate in days instead of months.

steps.title: Audit Content Gaps
steps.body: Run an AI SEO audit (e.g., Surfer or Clearscope) on your top 20 pages to surface missed keyword clusters and unanswered user questions. Export the suggestions to a shared spreadsheet and prioritize the top three gaps for immediate article creation.

steps.title: Generate Copy Variants
steps.body: Prompt GPT‑4 or Jasper with the identified gaps to produce five headline and three body‑copy variations each. Import the results into your A/B testing tool (Google Optimize, VWO) to start live testing within 48 hours.

steps.title: AI‑Boost Segmentation
steps.body: Export your CRM contact list to a CSV and run a clustering model in ChatGPT’s Code Interpreter or a no‑code AI platform like Obviously.ai. Use the generated segments to build a targeted email drip or paid‑media audience this week.

steps.title: Create AI Ad Assets
steps.body: Use DALL·E 3 or Midjourney to generate three visual concepts per product, then pair each with the copy variants from step 2. Upload the five best combos to Meta Ads Manager and set up automated rules for cost‑per‑click optimization.

steps.title: Deploy Predictive Dashboards
steps.body: Connect Google Analytics 4 and your ad platforms to Looker Studio, then add a BigQuery ML model that forecasts next‑week ROAS based on the new assets. Schedule a daily email with the dashboard link to keep the team aligned on performance.

pro_tips: Fine‑tune your AI prompts with brand‑specific terminology; a single keyword tweak can improve tone consistency by 30 %.
Always run AI‑generated copy through a quick human sanity check for factual accuracy before publishing.
Set a hard limit on AI‑generated ideas (e.g., max 10 per week) to avoid analysis paralysis and keep the creative pipeline flowing.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Sales
guides.intro: AI-driven prospect scoring and real-time conversation insights are cutting the sales cycle by up to 30% at leading B2B firms. By embedding large-language models into your CRM and outreach tools, reps get personalized scripts and data-driven pipeline forecasts without manual crunching.

steps.title: Integrate AI prospect scoring
steps.body: Connect an LLM-powered enrichment service (e.g., Clearbit + OpenAI) to your CRM to assign a confidence score to every new lead based on firmographics and recent intent signals. Export the score into your lead list so SDRs can prioritize the top‑10% each morning.

steps.title: Automate first‑touch email drafts
steps.body: Use a prompt template that feeds the lead’s score, industry, and pain points into a generative model (ChatGPT or Claude) to produce a 2‑sentence cold email. Paste the output into your outreach platform (Outreach, SalesLoft) and send; edit only if the tone feels off.

steps.title: Deploy AI call‑summary bots
steps.body: Run a real-time transcription service (e.g., Otter.ai) with a post-call LLM that extracts key objections, decision‑maker names, and next‑step dates, then logs them directly into the CRM record. This reduces manual note‑taking and ensures uniform data quality.

steps.title: Refresh pipeline forecasts
steps.body: Feed the latest opportunity data into a forecasting model built in Google Cloud AutoML or a pre‑built Salesforce Einstein add‑on, then compare the AI’s 30‑day close probability with your manual estimates. Adjust quotas or focus areas based on the divergence.

steps.title: Create AI win‑back sequences
steps.body: Identify churned accounts older than 90 days, run an LLM to craft a re‑engagement cadence that references prior purchase history and new product features, and load the sequence into your marketing automation tool. Track response rates to iterate the prompt.

pro_tips: Never let the model choose the subject line—run A/B tests on a static list of proven triggers and let AI only vary the body copy.
Lock the temperature parameter low (e.g., 0.2) for data-entry tasks; higher creativity is great for emails but kills consistency in CRM fields.
Schedule a weekly 15‑minute ‘AI audit’ where the team reviews a random sample of AI-generated notes to catch hallucinations before they corrupt reporting.

guides.title: How to Use AI in HR & People
guides.intro: Generative AI is now able to sift millions of CVs, craft interview scripts, and surface hidden performance patterns in real time. By embedding these models directly into your applicant tracking and talent management tools, HR teams are shaving days off hiring cycles and making data‑driven people decisions.

steps.title: Screen Resumes with AI
steps.body: Export the latest batch of applicants from Greenhouse and run them through an LLM‑based parser (e.g., OpenAI’s ChatGPT API with a custom resume‑extraction prompt). Tag each CV with skill scores and flag those that meet your minimum threshold for a human review.

steps.title: Create Structured Interview Guides
steps.body: Feed the job description into the Recruiter agent to generate a set of competency‑based questions that avoid gendered language. Export the list to your interview portal and assign each question to the appropriate interviewer before the interview slot.

steps.title: Personalize Onboarding Plans
steps.body: Upload new‑hire profiles into the Onboarding Specialist agent; it will stitch together a 30‑60‑90 plan that aligns role‑specific training, Slack channel introductions, and compliance tasks. Push the plan to your LMS (e.g., Workday Learning) so the employee sees a day‑by‑day agenda immediately.

steps.title: Launch AI‑Powered Performance Dashboards
steps.body: Connect the Performance Coach agent to your OKR tool (e.g., Asana) and HRIS to auto‑populate a dashboard with real‑time KPI variance, sentiment from quarterly check‑ins, and predictive risk scores for under‑performers. Share the link with managers for a weekly 10‑minute review.

steps.title: Deploy an HR FAQ Chatbot
steps.body: Train a lightweight chatbot on your employee handbook, benefits guide, and recent policy updates using the HR Analyst agent as a knowledge base. Embed the bot in Teams or your intranet, then monitor usage logs to refine answers over the next two weeks.

pro_tips: Run a pilot on a single department first and compare AI scores against actual hire success to calibrate thresholds.
Mask personally identifiable information before sending resumes to any LLM to stay compliant with GDPR and CCPA.
Keep a human‑in‑the‑loop for any performance‑risk alerts; AI flags patterns, but contextual nuance still needs a manager’s judgment.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Finance
guides.intro: AI is now turning weeks‑long month‑end close cycles into near‑real‑time processes by auto‑pulling data from ERP, bank feeds, and SaaS sources. It also generates variance explanations and scenario forecasts that senior leaders can act on within hours.

steps.title: Automate Data Ingestion
steps.body: Connect your ERP, payroll, and banking APIs to an AI‑powered ETL tool such as Azure Data Factory with built‑in GPT‑4 transforms. Let the model map source fields to your chart‑of‑accounts and flag mismatches before they land in the data lake.

steps.title: Deploy Generative Forecasting
steps.body: Feed the cleaned data into a time‑series model (e.g., Prophet or Azure AutoML) and use a LLM to translate the output into a quarterly revenue forecast spreadsheet. Schedule the run weekly so the forecast updates with the latest actuals without manual tweaking.

steps.title: Add AI‑driven Variance Alerts
steps.body: Set up a monitoring script that compares actuals vs. forecast, then prompts GPT‑4 to write a concise variance note with root‑cause hypotheses. Push the note to Slack or Teams for the finance lead to review before the next reporting cycle.

steps.title: Generate Narrative Reports
steps.body: Integrate the LLM with your BI tool (e.g., Tableau or Power BI) to auto‑populate the executive summary section of the month‑end deck. The model pulls key metrics, variance alerts, and scenario outcomes, producing a ready‑to‑present narrative in seconds.

steps.title: Integrate AI into Approval Workflow
steps.body: Embed the AI‑generated variance notes into your ERP’s approval screen using a simple webhook. Approvers see the rationale and suggested corrective actions, reducing back‑and‑forth email loops and speeding sign‑off.

pro_tips: Start with a single KPI (e.g., cash‑conversion cycle) to prove ROI before scaling AI across the board.
Fine‑tune the LLM on your company's historical commentary so the language mirrors your internal style and avoids generic filler.
Version‑control your AI prompts and data pipelines in Git; a minor prompt change can rewrite entire forecasts.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Legal & Compliance
guides.intro: AI is now fast‑tracking contract review, automating privacy‑rights workflows, and surfacing compliance gaps before they become issues. In a single week you can embed generative and classification models into the core legal processes that currently rely on manual checking.

steps.title: Run AI Contract Scan
steps.body: Upload your latest batch of NDAs, MSAs, and vendor agreements into the contract‑manager agent and let it flag missing clauses, unusual jurisdiction terms, and risk‑weighted language. Export the highlighted report to your contract repository and assign remediation tasks to the responsible owners.

steps.title: Create Policy‑Change Alerts
steps.body: Feed your internal policy documents into the compliance‑officer agent and set up keyword monitors for regulatory terms (e.g., "SARS‑CoV-2", "carbon‑credit"). The agent will push Slack notifications whenever a newly published regulation matches a policy gap.

steps.title: Automate Data‑Subject Requests
steps.body: Integrate the privacy‑officer agent with your ticketing system (Jira, ServiceNow) so incoming GDPR or CCPA requests are auto‑categorized, assigned a SLA, and pre‑filled with template response drafts. Review the AI‑generated draft, approve, and the system will dispatch the final reply.

steps.title: Draft Regulatory Filings
steps.body: Use the regulatory‑analyst agent to pull the latest filing templates from your industry regulator’s portal, then feed in the quarterly data set. The agent produces a first‑draft filing that you only need to verify numbers and sign off, cutting drafting time by up to 70%.

steps.title: Score Emerging Risks
steps.body: Run the risk‑manager agent over internal audit logs and external news feeds to produce a risk‑heat map for the next 30 days. Prioritize the top three scores, assign owners, and schedule a quick risk‑review meeting before the next board call.

pro_tips: Always keep a human reviewer in the loop for any AI‑generated clause changes—legal nuance still beats pattern matching.
Version‑control AI outputs in the same system you store originals; it makes rollback and audit trails effortless.
Fine‑tune the agents on your own contract and policy corpus; a small custom dataset improves relevance dramatically.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Operations
guides.intro: AI is turning operational bottlenecks into data‑driven opportunities by automatically surfacing waste, predicting demand spikes, and monitoring vendor performance in real time. In practice, AI tools can be layered onto existing SOPs and dashboards to shave hours off routine work each week.

steps.title: Map Processes with AI Mining
steps.body: Connect your ERP or ticketing system to an AI‑powered process‑mining tool (e.g., Celonis, UiPath Process Mining) and generate a visual map of actual transaction flows. Export the model to a shared drive so the Ops team can pinpoint deviations from the documented SOPs.

steps.title: Automate Repetitive Tasks
steps.body: Identify high‑frequency manual steps (invoice entry, status updates) and build RPA bots using platforms like Automation Anywhere or Make.com. Deploy the bots in a sandbox, run a pilot on one business unit, and measure time saved before scaling.

steps.title: Add AI Demand Forecasts
steps.body: Feed historical sales and inventory data into a cloud AI service (e.g., Amazon Forecast or Azure AI) to generate short‑term demand predictions. Import the forecasts into your S&OP spreadsheet to adjust reorder points instantly.

steps.title: Monitor Vendor SLAs with AI
steps.body: Configure an AI‑driven monitoring script (Python + OpenAI embeddings) to scan vendor portals and email receipts for SLA breaches. Set up Slack or Teams alerts so the Vendor Manager can intervene before penalties accrue.

steps.title: Embed AI Insights in Ops Dashboards
steps.body: Create a Power BI or Looker dashboard that pulls anomaly scores, bot performance metrics, and forecast accuracy into a single view. Schedule a 15‑minute daily stand‑up to review the AI alerts and assign corrective actions.

pro_tips: Start with a single, high‑volume process; the ROI of AI shines when you can quantify minutes saved per transaction.
Give the RPA bot a human fallback channel (e.g., a Teams button) to handle edge‑cases without breaking the workflow.
Re‑train your demand models quarterly with the latest market events; stale models generate more noise than insight.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Customer Support
guides.intro: AI is now routing tickets in seconds, tagging sentiment, and suggesting answers while agents stay on the line. These capabilities cut first‑response time and lift CSAT without adding headcount.

steps.title: Deploy AI ticket triage
steps.body: Connect an LLM‑backed bot (e.g., OpenAI with Zendesk webhook) to your inbox. Configure it to read incoming tickets, assign priority, and tag the appropriate product line within the first minute.

steps.title: Add real‑time sentiment scores
steps.body: Enable a sentiment‑analysis model on the ticket body and surface a green/yellow/red flag on the agent’s dashboard. Use the flag to route negative tickets to senior reps instantly.

steps.title: Generate draft replies automatically
steps.body: Set up a shortcut that sends the ticket text to an LLM prompt and returns a concise draft with blanks for personal details. Agents edit the draft, approve, and send—cutting typing time by up to 40%.

steps.title: Feed AI insights into the knowledge base
steps.body: Create a nightly job that extracts unresolved questions and the LLM’s most‑used answers, then pushes them into Confluence or Zendesk Guide as draft articles. Review and publish within 24 hours to improve self‑service deflection.

steps.title: Run a weekly AI performance review
steps.body: Pull metrics (first‑response time, CSAT lift, escalation rate) from your ticketing system and compare them against the AI‑enabled workflow. Adjust prompts, retrain on new tickets, and document changes in a runbook.

pro_tips: Keep LLM prompts under 150 tokens; longer prompts increase latency and hallucination risk.
Create a ‘human‑in‑the‑loop’ flag for any AI‑generated reply containing code or policy language.
Refresh the fine‑tuning data set every month with the latest top‑10 ticket clusters to keep suggestions relevant.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Product
guides.intro: AI is turning product teams into rapid‑iteration machines by turning raw data and sketches into actionable insights in minutes. Large language models now draft roadmaps, surface hidden usage patterns, and keep release communication crisp, letting you ship faster.

steps.title: Automate backlog prioritization
steps.body: Export your Jira or ClickUp backlog to CSV, feed it into an LLM with a prompt that scores each item on impact, effort, and strategic fit, and have the model output a ranked list. Import the ranked list back into the tool to instantly reorder work for the next sprint.

steps.title: Generate persona drafts
steps.body: Pull the latest survey and Mixpanel cohort data, then prompt an LLM to synthesize key demographics, goals, and pain points into 1‑page personas. Use the output as a starting point and validate with a quick 3‑person interview.

steps.title: Run AI‑augmented funnel analysis
steps.body: Connect Amplitude or Looker data to a LLM via an API, ask it to identify drop‑off stages and suggest hypotheses for the top three leaks. Export the suggestions to your product‑analytics board for immediate experiment planning.

steps.title: Draft AI release notes
steps.body: Feed the pull‑request diff and changelog entries into a fine‑tuned LLM that follows your style guide, then generate a release‑note draft in markdown. Paste the result into your release manager’s pipeline and do a quick copy‑edit before publishing.

steps.title: Deploy AI test monitoring
steps.body: Set up a webhook that streams A/B test results (e.g., from Optimizely) to a LLM which flags statistically significant changes and drafts a short insights blurb. Push that blurb into your Slack #product‑insights channel for real‑time stakeholder updates.

pro_tips: Always run a sanity check on AI‑generated rankings with a senior PM before committing to sprint planning.
Combine two prompt variants (e.g., impact‑first and effort‑first) and merge the results to reduce single‑model bias.
Version‑control your prompts in the same repo as your product docs so you can revert or iterate quickly.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Design & Creative
guides.intro: AI‑driven generators now turn sketchy ideas into ready‑to‑use wireframes, color systems, and copy within minutes. By plugging these outputs straight into Figma or Storybook you can shave days off a sprint without sacrificing quality.

steps.title: Generate Wireframes with Prompts
steps.body: Use a Figma AI plugin (e.g., Figmatic) or a text‑to‑image model to describe screen layout and export the result as SVG. Drop the SVG into your file and replace empty containers with real components.

steps.title: Extract Color Palettes Automatically
steps.body: Feed brand assets into an image‑analysis model (like Adobe Firefly) to pull primary, secondary, and accent colors. Save the output as design tokens in your style dictionary for immediate reuse.

steps.title: Create Component Variants via Codegen
steps.body: Prompt GPT‑4 with component specs (props, states, accessibility notes) and let it emit React/Vue code plus Figma variant descriptions. Copy the code into Storybook and sync the variant data back to your design file.

steps.title: Generate Microcopy in Context
steps.body: Run the copywriter agent on each UI element, supplying tone‑of‑voice and user‑flow details. Paste the resulting microcopy into your prototype to test clarity before hand‑off.

steps.title: Run AI Accessibility Audits
steps.body: Upload your Figma frames to an AI audit tool (e.g., axe AI) to catch contrast, focus order, and ARIA issues. Fix the flagged items directly in the file, then re‑run to confirm compliance.

pro_tips: Version‑control your prompts in a shared doc so you can reproduce a specific result later.
Treat AI‑generated assets as rough drafts; always apply your design system's token constraints before final approval.
Combine AI color extraction with manual palette refinement to avoid overly saturated or brand‑inconsistent shades.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Data & Analytics
guides.intro: AI is now stitching together raw data, feature engineering, and visual insights in minutes rather than weeks. By automating metadata extraction, code generation, and model monitoring, teams can ship analytics products faster while keeping governance tight.

steps.title: Catalog Sources with LLMs
steps.body: Run an AI‑powered data catalog scan (e.g., Azure Purview with built‑in LLM tagging) across your lake or warehouse. Export the generated JSON metadata and feed it into your dbt source definitions.

steps.title: Generate SQL Boilerplate
steps.body: Prompt a local LLM with your schema and a business question to receive ready‑to‑run SELECT statements. Paste the result into your notebook, run a quick sanity check, and commit the query to version control.

steps.title: Create Predictive Features
steps.body: Feed the curated tables into an AutoML platform like H2O AutoML or Vertex AI to auto‑create feature pipelines. Schedule the resulting feature‑store update as an Airflow DAG that runs nightly.

steps.title: Build AI‑Enhanced Dashboards
steps.body: Connect Power BI or Looker to the feature store and model predictions, then add AI visuals such as key‑driver analysis. Publish the report and configure a 24‑hour refresh to keep insights current.

steps.title: Set Up Model Monitoring
steps.body: Deploy Evidently AI within Prometheus to track data drift, performance decay, and bias. Create Slack alerts for threshold breaches and schedule a monthly review cycle.

pro_tips: Include explicit schema details in every LLM prompt; it cuts hallucinations dramatically.
Treat AI‑generated code like any other artifact: store it in Git, run linting, and run unit tests before production.
Use a lightweight embedding store (e.g., Qdrant) for internal documentation RAG, so analysts can ask unrestricted natural‑language queries without leaving the data environment.

guides.title: How to Use AI in Executive & Strategy
guides.intro: AI is now a real-time analyst for C-suite teams, turning raw market data, internal metrics, and competitor moves into instantly digestible insights. Executives can use generative models to prototype strategic options and surface risks within hours instead of weeks.

steps.title: Harvest Competitive Data
steps.body: Connect an AI-powered search tool like AlphaSense or MarketMuse to your Slack channel and run a daily query for key competitor announcements. Export the top five results to a shared Google Sheet, tagging each with source and sentiment for quick board briefings.

steps.title: AI-Driven Scenario Modeling
steps.body: Use a spreadsheet add-on (e.g., GPT for Sheets) to feed macro variables (interest rates, supply-chain latency) into a GPT-generated scenario table. The model will auto-calculate projected revenue ranges, letting you compare three-plus futures before the next strategy meeting.

steps.title: Summarize KPI Dashboards
steps.body: Upload the monthly KPI PDF to a summarization engine such as Claude or ChatGPT’s file-analysis mode and ask for a one-page executive summary with variance explanations. Paste the output into your board deck to cut prep time by 70%.

steps.title: Create an AI Decision Log
steps.body: Set up a Notion database with a GPT-backed “rationale” field that auto-writes a two-sentence justification whenever you log a decision. Include a confidence score generated from sentiment analysis of the discussion transcript.

steps.title: Run a Rapid Strategy Sprint
steps.body: Prompt the strategy-consultant agent with your top business objective and ask for three differentiated strategic options, each with pros, cons, and implementation milestones. Share the options in a Teams poll, collect votes, and schedule a 30-minute alignment call.

pro_tips: Never rely on a single AI output; always triangulate with a domain expert or recent earnings call transcript.
Store prompt versions in a Git repo so you can revert to a known-good prompt when the model drifts.
Assign a "AI champion" on the executive team to audit data bias weekly; this prevents strategic blind spots from creeping in.
